nodes.name: Bank Customer
nodes.description: Retail banking customer who initiates payments, views accounts, and receives notifications

nodes.name: Mobile Banking App
nodes.description: iOS and Android React Native application used by customers to access banking services

nodes.name: Web Banking Portal
nodes.description: React web application providing full banking functionality via browser

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.description: Single entry point for all client applications. Validates JWT tokens via auth-service, applies rate limiting, and proxies requests to downstream services by path prefix

nodes.name: Auth Service
nodes.description: Handles user authentication and JWT lifecycle. Issues access tokens (30min) and refresh tokens (7d). Sessions stored in Redis for fast invalidation

nodes.name: Accounts Service
nodes.description: Manages bank accounts, balances, and transaction ledger. Publishes account.updated events to Kafka on every balance change

metadata.technology: Java 21 + Spring Boot 3 + Spring Data JPA + Spring Kafka

nodes.name: Payments Service
nodes.description: Initiates and tracks payment transactions. Calls the SWIFT/ACH rail synchronously (critical path — no circuit breaker). Publishes payment lifecycle events to Kafka

metadata.risk: HIGH — synchronous external call to swift-ach-rail with no fallback. Latency spikes propagate directly to user-facing payment flow.

nodes.name: Fraud Detection Service
nodes.description: Consumes payment.initiated events from Kafka and runs real-time fraud scoring using graph queries on Neo4j. Detects circular transactions, velocity anomalies, and new-payee risks. Publishes fraud.alert events when score exceeds threshold

nodes.name: Customer Service
nodes.description: Manages customer profiles, KYC documents, and identity records. Stores unstructured profile data in MongoDB. Fetches linked accounts from accounts-service

nodes.name: Notification Service
nodes.description: Background Kafka consumer that dispatches email and SMS notifications based on domain events. Routes payment.completed to SendGrid, fraud.alert to Twilio SMS, account.updated to email digest

metadata.technology: Python 3.12 + kafka-python + SendGrid SDK + Twilio SDK
metadata.note: No inbound HTTP — pure event-driven consumer

nodes.name: Reporting Service
nodes.description: GraphQL API for analytics and audit reporting. Aggregates data across PostgreSQL (transactions, balances) and MongoDB (customer profiles)

metadata.technology: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + Strawberry GraphQL + asyncpg + motor

nodes.name: Auth PostgreSQL
nodes.description: PostgreSQL database storing user credentials (bcrypt hashed), audit login log, and account lock state

nodes.name: Bank PostgreSQL
nodes.description: PostgreSQL database storing accounts, transaction ledger, and payment orders. Shared between accounts-service and payments-service via separate schemas

nodes.name: Payments Schema (Bank PostgreSQL)
nodes.description: The payments schema within the shared Bank PostgreSQL instance. Logically separate but physically co-located with accounts schema

metadata.note: Same physical DB as postgres-accounts-db; separated by schema

nodes.name: Redis Session Cache
nodes.description: In-memory store for JWT refresh tokens and session state. Used by auth-service for fast token validation and invalidation

nodes.name: Customer MongoDB
nodes.description: Document store for customer profiles, KYC documents, and identity metadata. Schema-flexible to accommodate varying KYC requirements across jurisdictions

nodes.name: Neo4j Fraud Graph
nodes.description: Graph database storing entity relationships for fraud detection. Nodes are accounts, devices, and IP addresses. Edges represent transactions, logins, and shared attributes. Cypher queries detect circular payment rings, velocity anomalies, and shared-device patterns

nodes.name: Apache Kafka
nodes.description: Event streaming backbone. All async communication between services flows through Kafka topics. Topics: payment.initiated, payment.completed, payment.failed, fraud.alert, account.updated

nodes.name: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
nodes.description: Production AKS cluster hosting all banking microservices in the banking-system namespace. Network policies restrict service-to-service traffic by label selector

nodes.name: SWIFT/ACH Payment Rail
nodes.description: External payment rail for initiating ACH and SWIFT wire transfers. Called synchronously by payments-service on the critical payment path. Mocked locally at port 9999

metadata.contract: POST /v2/transfers — { amount, currency, source_account, destination_account, idempotency_key }
metadata.risk: HIGH — external dependency, no circuit breaker, failures propagate to user as 500

nodes.name: SendGrid
nodes.description: Transactional email provider. Used by notification-service to dispatch payment confirmations and account alerts

nodes.name: Twilio
nodes.description: SMS provider. Used by notification-service to send fraud alerts and OTP verification codes

relationships.description: Bank customer accesses services via mobile banking app
Mobile app connects to API gateway over HTTPS
Web app connects to API gateway over HTTPS
API gateway proxies /api/auth/* requests to auth-service and calls /auth/verify to validate every inbound JWT
API gateway proxies /api/accounts/* requests to accounts-service
API gateway proxies /api/payments/* requests to payments-service
API gateway proxies /api/customers/* requests to customer-service
API gateway proxies /api/reports/* requests to reporting-service
Auth service reads and writes user credentials and audit log to PostgreSQL
Auth service stores JWT refresh tokens and session state in Redis for fast validation and invalidation
Accounts service reads and writes account balances and transaction ledger to PostgreSQL
Accounts service publishes account.updated events to Kafka after every balance change
Payments service writes payment orders and status updates to PostgreSQL
Payments service publishes payment lifecycle events (initiated, completed, failed) to Kafka

Payments service calls the SWIFT/ACH rail synchronously to initiate external transfers. This is on the critical payment path with no circuit breaker — failures are propagated directly to the caller as 500 errors

metadata.failure-mode: hard-fail — no retry at this layer, no circuit breaker
metadata.breaking-change-risk: HIGH — idempotency_key must be unique per retry; path version changes break all payments

relationships.description: Fraud detection service consumes payment.initiated from Kafka and publishes fraud.alert when score exceeds threshold
Fraud detection service queries Neo4j via Bolt protocol to run graph pattern detection (circular transactions, shared devices, velocity checks)
Customer service reads and writes customer profiles and KYC documents to MongoDB
Customer service fetches linked account IDs from accounts-service via synchronous HTTP call
Notification service consumes multiple Kafka topics to trigger email and SMS dispatches
Notification service calls SendGrid API to send transactional emails (payment confirmations, account alerts)
Notification service calls Twilio to send SMS alerts for fraud events and OTP codes
Reporting service queries PostgreSQL for transaction history and account balances to generate reports
Reporting service queries MongoDB for customer profile data to enrich reports
All application services are deployed in the AKS cluster under the banking-system namespace
Infrastructure components (databases, Kafka, Redis) run as StatefulSets in the same AKS cluster

flows.name: Customer Login
flows.description: Customer authenticates via mobile/web app, receives JWT access and refresh tokens

transitions.description: Customer opens app and enters credentials
App sends POST /api/auth/login to API gateway
Gateway proxies to auth-service POST /auth/login
Auth service validates credentials against PostgreSQL users table
Auth service stores refresh token in Redis with TTL

flows.name: Payment Initiation
flows.description: Customer initiates a payment — the critical path that touches payments-service and the external SWIFT rail

transitions.description: Customer submits POST /api/payments from mobile app
Gateway validates JWT via GET /auth/verify
Gateway proxies to payments-service POST /payments
Payments service writes payment_order with status=pending
Payments service publishes payment.initiated event to Kafka
Payments service calls SWIFT/ACH rail POST /v2/transfers (synchronous, blocking)
On SWIFT success: payments service publishes payment.completed; on failure: payment.failed

flows.name: Real-Time Fraud Detection
flows.description: Triggered asynchronously by payment.initiated event — fraud score is computed and alert raised if above threshold

transitions.description: Fraud service consumes payment.initiated from Kafka
Fraud service runs Cypher queries against Neo4j graph to detect patterns
If fraud score > threshold, publishes fraud.alert to Kafka

flows.name: Payment Confirmation Notification
flows.description: After payment completes, notification-service sends confirmation email to customer

transitions.description: Notification service consumes payment.completed from Kafka
Notification service calls SendGrid to dispatch confirmation email

flows.name: Account Summary
flows.description: Customer views account balance and recent transactions via web portal

transitions.description: Web app sends GET /api/accounts/{id}/balance
Gateway validates JWT
Gateway proxies to accounts-service
Accounts service queries PostgreSQL for balance and recent transactions
